div1.entryFree:
Consul, 1) the chief magistrate of ancient Rome: Lucr. Arg. Oth. IV, 1, 102 Cor. II, 1, 238. 248 etc. Ant. I, 4, 58.
2) senator: the bookish theoric, wherein the tongued --s can propose as masterly as he, Oth. I, 1, 25. many of the --s are at the duke's already, I, 2, 43. Cymb. IV, 2, 385.
